name: Secrets of Fat-Free Italian Cooking
recipeIngredient: 2/3 cup fat-free ricotta cheese blended with 1/3 cup skim milk
recipeInstructions:
1. Use nonfat (or fat-free) Parmesan cheese in lasagna and other pasta dishes to save 100 calories and 25 grams of fat per 1 cup.
2. Add the nonfat Parmesan to canned low-fat cream soups and sauces for thickness and richness, saving an additional 100 calories.
3. Use ricotta mixture (part skim or fat-free) in sauces; cook over low heat until warmed through to prevent separation.
4. The ricotta mixture saves a significant amount of calories:
- Cream: 582.6 calories, 59.8 grams fat
- Low-fat ricotta mixture: 354.9 calories, 13.2 grams fat
- Fat-free ricotta and skim milk: 148.0 calories, 0.2 grams fat each
Available on-line at  > The Post and Courier, Charleston, SC: What's Cookin' Sunday, June 1, 1997. "How low can we go? As low (-fat) as you want ..." Posted by patHanneman on eat-lf
